zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Gascoigne IV於大約1426年出生在英國越可爾加福索普時，他的父親Sir William Gascoigne III24歲，母親Margaret Clarell31歲。他於大約1449年與Jane Neville結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1461年在他的家鄉享年37歲。

nameMeanings.name: Gascoigne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Anglo-Norman French Gascon, Middle English Gascoun, Gascon ‘Gascon, native of Gascony’ or the adjective Gascuinz ‘of Gascony’. The name of the region derives from that of the Basques, who are found close by and formerly extended into this region as well; they are first named in Roman sources as Vascōnes, but the original meaning of the name derived from a root eusk- in the non-Indo-European language that they still speak today, is completely obscure. By the Middle Ages the Basques had been displaced from most of Gascony by speakers of Gascon (a dialect of Occitan, related to French), who were proverbial for their boastfulness. In the 11th century Gascony united with Aquitaine and was thus held by England between 1154 and 1453.

variants.name: Gascon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jaime, Mario, Miguel, Rafael, Ruben, Amado, Ana, Ana Maria, Angel, Caridad.  French Andre, Chantal, Jean-Guy, Jean-Philippe, Monique, Serge, Simonne.
French; Spanish (Gascón): habitational name for someone from the province of Gascony, Old French Gascogne (see Gascoigne ). This surname is also found in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Gascoigne IV was born about 1426, in Gawthorpe, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir William Gascoigne III, was 24 and his mother, Margaret Clarell, was 31. He married Jane Neville about 1449. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died about 1461, in his hometown, at the age of 37.

Il a épousé Jane Neville environ 1449. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé environ 1461, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 37 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Gascoigne IV(이)가 대략 1426년 영국 요르크실르 가w소에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Gascoigne III(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Clarell(은)는 31세였다. 그는 대략 1449년 Jane Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1461년 그의 고향에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.
